senses.definition: Having a crust made of or resembling septaria (a type of rock with cracked, angular patterns).
examples: The septarian nodules embedded in the rock looked like mosaic puzzles.
Geologists described the specimen as septarian due to its cracked interior.

senses.definition: A term historically used to describe a group or grouping of seven; a septenary arrangement.
examples: The septariate structure divided the system into seven parts.
In old texts, the septariate was used to describe sevenfold divisions.

senses.definition: A structure or chamber containing seven parts; a sevenfold division or chamber.
examples: The ancient tomb featured a septarium with seven chambers.
Passepartout described a septarium as a seven-room gallery in certain temples.

senses.definition: Having a wall or partition dividing into compartments; intersected by cross walls.
examples: The septate basement was divided into several storage rooms.
A septate fungus forms into discrete, separated structures.

senses.definition: Having been divided into compartments or sections.
examples: The septated loaf was cut into even slices.
A septated cell wall shows clear internal divisions.

senses.definition: The process of dividing into compartments or sections.
examples: Septation of the colony occurred as nutrients became scarce.
During septation, the embryo forms distinct cellular boundaries.

senses.definition: Multiple septations; multiple divisions into compartments.
examples: The specimen showed several septations within each chamber.
In the fossil, several septations indicated complex internal structuring.

senses.definition: A rare or specialized term describing a septate and articulated structure (context-specific).
examples: The specimen was described as septatoarticulate in the monograph.
Septatoarticulate features were noted in the beetle's exoskeleton.

senses.definition: A technical term; likely a sevenfold or septa-related anatomical or structural descriptor.
examples: The septaugintal pattern was described in the anatomical atlas.
Septaugintal features appeared in the microscopic scan.

senses.definition: Having seven valence points or seven available bonding sites (chemistry).
examples: A septavalent atom can form seven chemical bonds.
The septavalent ion showed unusual coordination chemistry.

senses.definition: A sevenfold division or quantity; rarely used outside technical or historical contexts.
examples: The manuscript referenced a septave in the sevenfold scheme.
In some poets, a septave appears as a symbolic seven.

senses.definition: A period of 700 years; a seven-century anniversary.
examples: The museum celebrated the septcentenary of the town's founding.
A septcentenary anniversary was marked with exhibitions.

senses.definition: Surgical procedures to remove septa (partitions) within an organ or structure.
examples: The patient underwent septectomies to relieve obstruction.
Septectomies are performed in certain complex cases.

senses.definition: Surgical removal of a septum or partitions within a structure.
examples: A septectomy was recommended to improve airflow.
The team discussed the risks of septectomy.

senses.definition: The ninth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
examples: Her birthday is in September.
The festival takes place every September.

senses.definition: A person born or associated with September; used rarely and informally.
examples: The Septemberer joked about back-to-school sales.
As a Septemberer, she loved autumn温.

senses.definition: A belief or tendency related to September; used humorously or descriptively.
examples: His septemberism leaned toward autumnal symbolism.
The article critiqued septemberism in media calendars.

senses.definition: A person who favors September, or who adheres to septemberism.
examples: The Septemberist preferred autumn-themed events.
As a Septemberist, she collected September calendars.

senses.definition: To designate or celebrate something in September; to mark with September features.
examples: The festival was septembrized with autumn decorations.
They septembrize the event to honor harvest traditions.

senses.definition: One who septembrizes; an agent or organizer of September-themed activities.
examples: The septembrizer organized the autumn festival.
As a septembrizer, he planned September campaigns.

senses.definition: A period of seven decades; a seventy-year span.
examples: The building has stood for septemdecenary years.
A septemdecenary anniversary was celebrated with archival exhibits.

senses.definition: A number equal to 10^60; a 1 followed by sixty zeros.
examples: In some mathematical discussions, the term septemdecillion is used for a very large quantity.
The computer scientists imagined data sets numbering in septemdecillions.
